goals: Pay off a dangerous debt before the next port cycle.
Find a missing relative lost in the outer system.
Secure enough credits to buy passage out of hostile territory.
Recover proof of a corporate crime and sell it to the right buyer.
Earn a permanent berth on a ship that can outrun their past.
Track down the person who ruined their old life.
Build a reputation strong enough to matter in the void.
Survive long enough to retire somewhere with real gravity.
Buy out the remainder of an indenture contract.
Find a ship willing to carry them under a false manifest.
Recover a family claim marker from a disputed asteroid.
Smuggle medicine into a quarantined habitat.
Expose a port official who has been selling emergency docking slots.
Earn enough hazard pay to get a sibling out of cold storage.
Locate a derelict vessel before the salvage courts assign ownership.
Find the captain who abandoned their crew during a burn window.
Pay for radiation treatment before the symptoms become visible.
Secure a legal identity after years of forged station papers.
Buy a sealed habitat room with a door that locks from the inside.
Repay a favor owed to someone who should have left them behind.
Get their name removed from a corporate blacklist.
Recover a stolen data core before its contents are decrypted.
Make one clean fortune and leave the system forever.
Track down a rumor of an unregistered fuel cache.
Win custody of a child or dependent trapped in station bureaucracy.
Find proof that a supposedly accidental hull breach was murder.
Gain enough status to negotiate instead of beg.
Deliver a message to someone who has been legally dead for years.
Find a safe port that will not ask questions about their past.
Complete one last contract before their body gives out.
Buy a proper burial plot under open sky.
Recover a ship transponder tied to an old betrayal.
Clear the debt ledger of their old crew.
Find the doctor who falsified their medical record.
Save enough to purchase a share in a working vessel.
Make their family name mean something outside one dying habitat.
Steal back a prototype before it disappears into corporate custody.
Keep a promise made during decompression alarms.
Find out why their old crew’s logs were erased.
Earn passage inward before the next fuel shortage traps them.
notes: Keeps a careful watch schedule and trusts written logs more than promises.
Carries old station habits into every room and checks exits first.
Speaks softly until the situation turns transactional.
Treats equipment maintenance as a private ritual.
Has a habit of memorizing docking codes, faces, and debts.
Avoids unnecessary attachments but remembers every favor.
Looks more comfortable in pressure gear than street clothes.
Keeps one personal keepsake sealed against vacuum and dust.
Checks pressure seals twice, even planetside.
Never sleeps with both ears uncovered.
Keeps emergency rations sorted by expiration date and emotional value.
Uses old ship slang from a crew that no longer exists.
Writes down every promise, payment, and threat in the same battered notebook.
Refuses to sit with their back to an airlock.
Can identify common stations by the taste of their recycled water.
Still flinches at decompression sirens.
Maintains their boots like they are life-support equipment.
Keeps a private map of safe bunks, cheap meals, and corrupt clerks.
Has opinions about docking clamps that sound almost religious.
Treats gravity like a luxury and silence like a warning.
Carries a vacuum-rated marker for labeling bodies, crates, and debts.
Does not trust anyone who wastes propellant casually.
Keeps an old port access badge long after it stopped working.
Measures time by burn windows, shift bells, and payment cycles.
Knows how to make bad coffee from worse water.
Keeps their suit patched past the point of dignity.
Always asks who profits before asking who is right.
Has a laugh that disappears the moment money is mentioned.
Can sleep through machinery but wakes instantly when it stops.
Keeps a list of ships they will never board again.
Collects tiny luxuries from places they hated.
Has a habit of touching bulkheads when nervous.
Does not raise their voice unless there is no atmosphere to waste.
Keeps counterfeit papers hidden inside something sentimental.
Treats medical scans as negotiations, not facts.
Always knows the nearest medkit, breaker panel, and exit route.
Has one story about a failed evacuation and never tells it the same way twice.
Carries spare filters for people they claim not to care about.
Never signs a contract without checking the death-benefit clause.
Knows the smell of overheated wiring better than perfume.
Keeps old debts separated into payable, impossible, and personal.
Talks to machines when tired and people when necessary.
Looks at clean gravity wells with suspicion.
Will share oxygen before sharing information.
Knows how to disappear during shift change.
Keeps a small stash of local currency from every port they survived.
Treats fresh fruit like contraband.
Still uses the emergency hand signals of an old crew.
Can judge a ship’s finances from the condition of its galley.
Does not believe in lucky charms, but carries three.
Feels uneasy in rooms without visible pipes, vents, or maintenance panels.
Has memorized too many last transmissions.
Keeps their personal effects packed as if departure could happen any minute.
